sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CORSAIR Debuts their new XENEON 32QHD165 Gaming Monitor, featuring an ultra-slim 32-inch QHD screen
feed.summary: CORSAIR has launched its new XENEON 32QHD165 Gaming Monitor, featuring a 32-inch QHD IPS LED screen with a 165Hz refresh rate and compatibility with AMD FreeSync Premium and NVIDIA G-SYNC. The monitor is designed for gamers and creators, offering excellent color accuracy, HDR400 support, and integration with CORSAIR iCUE and Elgato Stream Deck software. It also boasts a versatile design with a unique stand featuring a 1/4" threading for mounting accessories and comprehensive connectivity options.

feed.title: UK-based Ferroglobe eyes restart plan for silicon metal facility in Selma
feed.summary: Ferroglobe PLC's subsidiary, Globe Metallurgical Inc. (GMI), is planning to restart its silicon metal facility in Selma, Alabama, due to favorable market conditions. The company aims to resume operation of one furnace in December and a second by March 2022, creating about 100 new local jobs. This move is part of Ferroglobe's strategic turnaround plan and has the support of Alabama state officials, who view it as a significant economic boost for the region.

feed.title: Marvell Extends Data Infrastructure Leadership with TSMC 3nm Platform
feed.summary: Marvell announced its leadership in data infrastructure silicon by introducing a new advanced silicon platform utilizing TSMC's 3nm process technology. This platform offers superior power, performance, and area, enabling customers to develop unique, system-optimized solutions for various markets, including cloud data centers and 5G carriers. Marvell's comprehensive standards-based silicon platform integrates advanced die-to-die interface IP and TSMC's CoWoS packaging technology, providing cutting-edge multi-chip solutions.

feed.title: Integra LifeSciences enhances multicloud capability with OCI, achieves 90% time savings and boosts IT efficiency
feed.summary: Integra LifeSciences significantly improved its IT efficiency and multicloud capabilities by migrating its mission-critical E-Business Suite applications to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). This move resulted in a 90% time savings for daily sales invoice reports and a reduction of nightly data analytics processing from 8.5 to 3.5 hours, leveraging OCI's high-performance platform and its interconnection with Microsoft Azure. The company also enhanced business resiliency and gained proactive performance insights, solidifying a strategic position for future growth.

feed.title: RISR vs. VEGN: Head-To-Head ETF Comparison
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed head-to-head comparison of two ETFs, RISR (FolioBeyond Alternative Income and Interest Rate Hedge ETF) and VEGN (US Vegan Climate ETF). It covers various metrics including fees, performance, dividend yield, holdings (ESG scores are provided for VEGN), and technical indicators. The goal is to help investors make informed decisions by contrasting these ETFs.

feed.title: PCIe 5.0 controllers now available with zero-latency integrated IDE
feed.summary: Rambus Inc. has released Compute Express Link (CXL) 2.0 and PCI Express (PCIe) 5.0 controllers that feature integrated Integrity and Data Encryption (IDE) modules. These controllers offer zero-latency security, crucial for high-speed data center applications like AI/ML and HPC, by employing AES-GCM symmetric-key cryptographic block ciphers. This development, enhanced by the recent acquisition of PLDA, aims to protect against physical attacks on CXL and PCIe links while ensuring data confidentiality, integrity, and replay protection at full 32 GT/s speed.

feed.title: Oski Technology, an Expert in Formal Verification, Joins NVIDIA
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced the acquisition of Oski Technology, a company specializing in formal verification methods. This acquisition aims to significantly increase NVIDIA's investment and commitment to formal verification strategies, which are crucial for ensuring the correctness of complex modern processors and new autonomous machine applications. Oski, known for its leading-edge work and expertise, has been a long-time partner of NVIDIA, and most of its employees will join NVIDIA, expanding NVIDIA's engineering presence in India.

feed.title: Qualcomm Dual Station Wi-Fi 6E Tech Brings Wired Ping Times To Windows 11 Gamers
feed.summary: Qualcomm has partnered with Microsoft to bring Wi-Fi Dual Station technology to Windows 11, utilizing Qualcomm's FastConnect hardware to enable wired-like ping times for gamers over Wi-Fi. This technology leverages multiple Wi-Fi bands (2.4GHz, 5GHz, and 6GHz) simultaneously to reduce latency and packet loss. Valve is integrating support for this feature into its Steamworks SDK, ensuring popular titles like Dota 2 and Counter-Strike: Global Offensive can benefit from this enhanced wireless gaming experience.

feed.title: Chipmakers resist US bid for client data
feed.summary: Chipmakers in Taiwan and South Korea are resisting a request from the US Department of Commerce for sensitive client and supply chain data, aimed at resolving global chip shortages. TSMC has stated it will not hand over client information, while the South Korean Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy has expressed concerns about the request's scope. This resistance highlights the complexity of the issue, with Taiwanese politicians and individual investors also voicing opposition.

feed.title: Highest Density SuperBlade® Server for HPC Applications
feed.summary: Supermicro's SuperBlade® server solutions offer high density and efficiency for HPC, enterprise cloud, and other demanding applications. Available in 8U, 6U, and 4U enclosures, these systems support advanced processors from Intel and AMD, high-speed networking, and various storage options, alongside resource-saving architecture for optimal TCO. The article highlights successful deployments by companies like HEROZ and Korea Hydro and Nuclear Power, and provides extensive details on features and configurations.

feed.title: Synopsys announces industry's first complete High Bandwidth Memory Gen3 (HBM3) IP solution
feed.summary: Synopsys has announced the industry's first complete High Bandwidth Memory Gen3 (HBM3) IP solution, including controller, PHY, and verification IP for 2.5D multi-die package systems. This solution aims to meet the high-bandwidth and low-power memory requirements for high-performance computing, AI, and graphics applications. It promises up to 921 GB/s memory bandwidth, improved power efficiency, and accelerated verification closure for designers working with advanced SoC designs.

feed.title: Understanding the Buzz Around the BUZZ ETF
feed.summary: The article discusses the VanEck Vectors Social Sentiment ETF (BUZZ), which debuted in March 2021 and capitalizes on the "meme stock" phenomenon. It examines BUZZ's methodology, noting that its longer lookback period means it doesn't solely focus on highly speculative meme stocks like GameStop and AMC. Instead, BUZZ's portfolio includes quality names like AMD, Apple, Nvidia, and Pfizer, and exhibits low single-stock risk.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. stock outperforms market on strong trading day
feed.summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) rallied 3.92% on Wednesday, outperforming the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed below its 52-week high but outperformed competitors like NVIDIA Corp. and Intel Corp. Trading volume for AMD was slightly below its 50-day average.

feed.title: Rambus' RCD Chip Enables DDR5-5600 for Next-Gen Servers
feed.summary: Rambus has unveiled its second-generation registering clock driver (RCD) chip for DDR5 memory, which will allow memory module manufacturers to create registered DDR5 modules with data transfer rates up to 5600 MT/s for advanced servers. This new RCD offers a 17% higher peak bandwidth, optimized latencies for better real-world performance, and lower power consumption compared to its first-generation counterpart. The company anticipates that DDR5-5600 RDIMMs and LRDIMMs will become available as soon as compatible servers hit the market.

feed.title: AMD Radeon RX 6600 Graphics Card Delivers Incredible High-Refresh Rate 1080p Gaming
feed.summary: AMD has launched the Radeon RX 6600 graphics card, targeting the midrange market with high-refresh rate 1080p gaming experiences. This card utilizes the AMD RDNA 2 architecture, offering features like 32 MB of AMD Infinity Cache, 8GB of GDDR6 memory, and support for AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution and Smart Access Memory technology. It promises up to 1.3X better performance-per-watt than competitors in select titles at 1080p max settings, aiming to deliver enthusiast-level performance in a midrange solution.

feed.title: NXP selects AWS to host chip design & verification systems
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors has chosen AWS as its primary cloud provider for electronic design automation (EDA) workloads, migrating the majority of its EDA systems to AWS infrastructure. This move aims to leverage AWS's scalable compute power to accelerate chip design and verification processes, reduce costs, and minimize scheduling risks. The partnership expands on previous collaborations between NXP and AWS, focusing on accelerating semiconductor innovation and bringing new designs to market faster.

feed.title: Looking Ahead: High-Speed In-Vehicle Display and Sensor Connections
feed.summary: The automotive industry is experiencing an exponential increase in innovation, particularly with advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicles (AVs). This rapid progression necessitates high-speed, high-bandwidth in-vehicle networking, moving beyond traditional linear development. The article discusses the critical role of technologies like automotive Ethernet and emerging SerDes standards in supporting complex sensor and display data, alongside challenges in testing and ensuring interoperability for optimal performance and safety.

feed.title: ASML Holding: On Sentiment, Semiconductors & Sanity
feed.summary: This article discusses ASML Holding N.V. (Nasdaq: ASML), a critical player in the semiconductor equipment market, highlighting its unique extreme ultraviolet (EUV) and deep ultraviolet (DUV) lithography technologies. Despite strong fundamental performance, including significant revenue growth and high gross margins, the stock recently experienced a 20% drop due to negative investor sentiment and inflation fears, rather than any issues with its business operations. The author argues that Wall Street's current focus on sentiment over fundamentals presents a buying opportunity for growth investors.

feed.title: Apple unveils game-changing MacBook Pro
feed.summary: Apple has unveiled a completely reimagined MacBook Pro powered by the new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, available in 14- and 16-inch models. These new laptops offer extraordinary performance, unprecedented battery life, and advanced features like a Liquid Retina XDR display, improved camera and audio systems, and extensive connectivity, marking a significant leap for professional users. The MacBooks are designed for various professionals, offer remarkable power efficiency, and feature an environmentally conscious design with recycled materials.

feed.title: Alibaba unveils custom ARM-based server chip for cloud computing data centers
feed.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd has developed its own ARM-based server chip, the Yitian 710, for use in its data centers, signifying its continued push into semiconductor development. This move aligns with similar efforts by other global cloud providers like Amazon and Huawei, and supports the Chinese government's initiative to bolster its domestic chip industry. The chip, developed by Alibaba's T-Head unit, will not be commercially available outside the company, which is China's largest cloud computing provider.

feed.title: New RF FPGA solutions transform EW platforms
feed.summary: The article discusses how new RF FPGA solutions from Xilinx, specifically the RFSoC and Versal ACAP families, are transforming Electronic Warfare (EW) platforms. These highly integrated devices combine critical EW functions like data converters and powerful processors into single chips, significantly reducing size, weight, power, and cost (SWaP-C) while improving performance. This technological advancement enables new system architectures, addresses challenges in phased-array antennas, and supports advanced capabilities like AI for more agile and effective EW systems.

feed.title: NVIDIA Introduces the Next Generation in Cloud Gaming
feed.summary: NVIDIA has announced its next-generation cloud gaming platform, GeForce NOW, which will offer GeForce RTX 3080-class gaming performance through a new high-performance membership tier. This tier leverages NVIDIA's SuperPOD architecture, providing significant graphical horsepower and enabling high resolutions, fast frame rates, and low latency across various devices. The service provides access to over 1,100 games, including many popular free-to-play and upcoming titles with RTX ON.

feed.title: Beelink SER3 Review – A good AMD Ryzen 7 mini PC… after tweaks
feed.summary: This review examines the Beelink SER3 mini PC featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 3750H processor, highlighting that while its out-of-the-box performance is satisfactory, significant improvements can be achieved through BIOS tweaks, including memory overclocking and power scheme adjustments. The article details hardware specifications, performance benchmarks on Windows and Ubuntu, eGPU capabilities, power consumption, and gaming performance after optimization. It concludes that with a few specific changes, the SER3 offers impressive performance for its price, despite some initial limitations like single-channel memory.

feed.title: Diversification away from Taiwan chipmakers is necessary
feed.summary: The article discusses the global reliance on Taiwan's chipmakers, particularly TSMC, amidst a worldwide chip shortage. It highlights efforts by South Korea, China, and the U.S. (Intel, GlobalFoundries) to diversify semiconductor production, acknowledging the difficulties due to high capital investment and technological complexity. The author concludes that while diversification is necessary, it will be a challenging and time-consuming process given Taiwan's established and highly specialized chipmaking ecosystem.

feed.title: AMD sales rise 54% on strong demand for chips for servers and game consoles
feed.summary: AMD reported strong third-quarter earnings, beating analyst expectations for both revenue and EPS, driven by high demand for its chips in PCs, servers, and game consoles. The company also issued a robust forecast for the fourth quarter, expecting continued growth in server and semi-custom segments amidst ongoing chip shortages. AMD's strategic acquisition of Xilinx is proceeding, aiming to strengthen its position in the data center market.

feed.title: AMD Grows Revenue In Sixth Straight Quarter Reveal Q3 2021 Results
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) reported significant revenue growth for its third fiscal quarter of 2021, marking its sixth consecutive quarter of revenue increase, driven primarily by its Enterprise, Embedded and Semi-custom segment. The company recorded $4.3 billion in net sales, representing a 54% annual and 12% sequential growth, with strong contributions from data center and custom-design chips. Despite a sequential drop in cash flows due to investments in long-term supply chain capacity, AMD doubled its operating income and achieved a 78% annual net income growth, with projections for continued revenue and gross margin improvement in the current quarter.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics' STM32 Ecosystem Extensions Kick-Start Development with STM32U5 MCUs
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has launched STM32Cube software packs, tools, and evaluation boards to accelerate development with its latest STM32U5 microcontrollers (MCUs). The STM32U5 MCUs, now available for mass-market applications, combine enhanced security and power-saving features with the Arm Cortex-M33 core, making them suitable for connected consumer products, smart-home devices, and industrial applications. The comprehensive ecosystem includes over 200 project examples, various RTOS support, and robust cybersecurity features to aid development and certification processes.

feed.title: Intel 12th Gen Core Alder Lake Details at Intel Innovation 2021
feed.summary: Intel unveiled its 12th Gen Core Alder Lake architecture at Intel Innovation 2021, marking a significant architectural shift with a hybrid design combining performance (P-cores) and efficient (E-cores) cores. The platform also introduces support for DDR5 and PCIe Gen5, aiming to enhance multi-tasking performance and scalability across various form factors. This announcement sets the stage for a new generation of Intel processors focusing on diverse workloads and improved efficiency.

feed.title: Chipmaker GlobalFoundries valued at $26 bln in lackluster Nasdaq debut
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries Inc. made a lackluster Nasdaq debut, with shares opening flat and finishing slightly below its IPO price, valuing the chipmaker at approximately $26 billion. Despite raising $2.6 billion, the company faces lower gross margins compared to competitors, though it plans significant investments to boost capacity and future margins amid a global chip shortage. CEO Thomas Caulfield expects demand to remain high for years, with acute shortages potentially easing in 2023.

feed.title: ECE alumnus Kevin Lepak receives one of chip maker AMD’s highest honors
feed.summary: Kevin Lepak, an alumnus of Electrical & Computer Engineering at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, has been appointed as an AMD Corporate Fellow, one of the company's highest honors. This recognition is for his critical role in designing AMD's next-generation processors, including EPYC CPUs and Instinct accelerators, and his leadership in system-on-chip design and memory performance. Lepak's contributions have significantly impacted AMD's leadership in the data center market and its high-performance product roadmap.
feed.source: College of Engineering | University of Wisconsin-Madison
feed.source_domain: College of Engineering | University of Wisconsin-Madison

feed.title: Startup Rips The Switch Out Of High Performance Networks
feed.summary: Traditional networking infrastructures are struggling to keep up with the demands of modern applications like AI and the rapid movement of data to the cloud. This has led to innovative approaches from companies like Aruba Networks, leveraging Pensando's DPU, and Rockport Networks, which completely redesigns network architecture by removing switches. Rockport's approach, using its rNOS and NC1225 cards with passive optical cables, aims to significantly improve performance, bandwidth, and latency, especially for east-west traffic prevalent in HPC and AI workloads.

feed.title: Microsoft Cloud at Ignite 2021: Metaverse, AI and hyperconnectivity in a hybrid world
feed.summary: Microsoft unveiled over 90 new services and updates at Ignite 2021, focusing on the metaverse, AI breakthroughs, and building a trust fabric. Key announcements include Dynamics 365 Connected Spaces and Mesh for Microsoft Teams to power metaverse experiences, and the Azure OpenAI Service for enterprise-grade AI. The company emphasizes enabling hybrid work, hyperconnected businesses, and end-to-end security through these innovations.

feed.title: American chip giants raise prices again to simulate the upsurge of IC prices
feed.summary: American chip giant ADI, facing pressure from rising supply chain costs, particularly wafer manufacturing, is set to increase product prices again starting December 5, 2021. This follows a previous price hike in early April and mirrors similar actions by competitors like Texas Instruments and On Semet. The price increases are boosting A-share analog chip companies, though China's analog chip market still has low self-sufficiency and high growth potential for domestic manufacturers.

feed.title: India to push manufacturing of semiconductors
feed.summary: India plans to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing with significant capital and production-linked incentives. This initiative, driven by the Prime Minister's Office, aims to address the global chip shortage exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and geopolitical tensions, which severely impacted industries like the auto sector. The government seeks to attract investors by providing financial support and tax reductions, anticipating a surge in semiconductor demand as India's electronics production rapidly grows.

feed.title: AMD Radeon™ PRO V620 GPU Delivers Powerful, Multi-Purpose Data Center Visual Performance for Today’s Demanding Cloud Workloads
feed.summary: AMD announced the Radeon PRO V620 GPU, leveraging the RDNA™ 2 architecture to provide high-performance acceleration for demanding cloud workloads such as AAA gaming, 3D applications, and office productivity. This GPU features innovative partitioning, multi-stream encoders, and 32GB GDDR6 memory, ensuring cost-effective graphics for various use cases. Designed to support advanced virtualization and modern APIs, the Radeon PRO V620 aims to redefine cloud gaming and professional graphics in data centers.

feed.title: Intel Loses Ground to AMD as Demand for Chromebook Chips Wanes
feed.summary: Intel Corp. lost over 2 percentage points of market share to Advanced Micro Devices Inc. in the third quarter of 2021, a setback driven primarily by a sharp decline in demand for chips used in low-end notebooks like Chromebooks. AMD now accounts for 24.6% of personal-computer processor sales, up from 22.5% a year prior, as Intel's technology edge erodes.

feed.title: GOI to launch a mega-billion plan for semiconductor manufacturers
feed.summary: The Indian government is set to introduce a significant capital and production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing, addressing global chip shortages. This initiative involves discussions with major global chipmakers like TSMC and Intel, offering financial support, tariff cuts, and leveraging programs like SPECS. The move aims to capitalize on rising domestic demand, project electronic production to reach $350-400 billion by 2025, and attract investments following China's "Plus One" policy.

feed.title: Europe Photomask Market New Study Offers Insights up to 2028|
feed.summary: This article announces a new study on the Europe Photomask Market, offering insights up to 2028. Key players like Toppan Photomasks and Dai Nippon Printing Co. Ltd. are highlighted. The report covers growth elements, trends, forecasts, size, share, demand, and distribution, with a focus on the impact of IoT and semiconductor demands.

feed.title: AMD shares close up 10% after company wins Meta partnership, announces new chips
feed.summary: AMD shares surged 10% after the company announced a partnership with Meta (formerly Facebook) as a chip customer and unveiled a new range of chips at its Accelerated Data Center Premiere event. This move strengthens AMD's position against competitors like Intel, as the company continues to gain market share with its data center chip sales experiencing significant growth. Additional details about the Meta partnership are expected to be revealed later in the week at the Open Compute Project summit.

feed.title: New AMD Instinct™ MI200 Series Accelerators Bring Leadership HPC and AI Performance to Power Exascale Systems and More
feed.summary: AMD has announced its new Instinct MI200 series accelerators, including the MI250X, which are designed to deliver exascale-class performance for HPC and AI workloads. These accelerators feature the AMD CDNA 2 architecture, a multi-die GPU design with 128GB of HBM2e memory, and achieve a significant performance advantage over competing data center accelerators. The MI200 series, combined with 3rd Gen AMD EPYC CPUs and the ROCm 5.0 software platform, aims to accelerate scientific discovery and will power the Frontier supercomputer.

feed.title: AMD Blitzes Intel, Nvidia With New Faster EPYC, Instinct Chips
feed.summary: AMD announced new EPYC CPUs and Instinct GPUs aimed at challenging Intel and Nvidia in data center workloads for high-performance computing and AI. The new EPYC processors (Milan-X) feature 3D chiplet technology to triple L3 cache, dramatically increasing performance, especially for technical computing. The Instinct MI200 series GPUs are positioned as "the world's fastest HPC and AI accelerator," promising up to 4.9 times faster HPC and 20 percent faster AI performance compared to Nvidia’s A100 GPU.

feed.title: Intel Pays VIA $125M For Centaur Technology's x86 Design Team
feed.summary: Intel has reportedly paid VIA $125 million for unspecified assets related to Centaur Technology, with strong indications that the deal is for the right to recruit Centaur's engineering team rather than their intellectual property or brands. This move comes as Intel aggressively recruits talent for its rejuvenation plans. While VIA will retain the right to build x86 CPUs, Centaur's website is down, suggesting a shift in their consumer-facing presence.

feed.title: AMD lands Meta as customer and takes on Nvidia, sending shares up 11%
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares rose 11% after announcing Meta Platforms Inc. as a new data center chip customer, intensifying its competition with Intel. AMD also unveiled new chips, including the MI200 for AI and machine learning to challenge Nvidia, and the Bergamo processor for cloud computing to compete with Ampere Computing. These strategic moves bolster AMD's market position against larger rivals and secure its presence with major cloud providers.

feed.title: Supermicro Enhances Broadest Portfolio of Edge to Cloud AI Systems with Accelerated Inferencing and New Intelligent Fabric Support
feed.summary: Supermicro announced enhancements to its AI GPU server portfolio, integrating new NVIDIA Ampere-family GPUs to deliver up to 10x faster AI inference performance and 3x faster training. These systems are designed for a wide range of applications from edge computing to cloud environments, critical for uses like autonomous driving, video streaming, and cloud gaming. Supermicro also introduced its Universal GPU Platform and systems supporting the new NVIDIA Quantum-2 InfiniBand product line, alongside optimized solutions for NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise.

feed.title: CAE Simulation Software Market to Show Strong Growth | Leading
feed.summary: The CAE Simulation Software Market is projected to experience strong growth, driven by increasing demand for product design assessments and simulation for manufacturing optimization, particularly in industries like automotive. North America is expected to lead this growth due to a high number of SMEs and focus on R&D. Key players in this market include ANSYS, Siemens, CAE Simulation and Solutions, and Dassault Systemes.

feed.title: AMD wins Meta as a data centre customer
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has secured Meta (formerly Facebook) as a data centre chip customer, leading to an 11% surge in AMD's shares. This win highlights AMD's growing market presence against competitors like Nvidia and Intel, especially with the launch of new chips like the MI200 for AI and machine learning, and "Bergamo" for cloud computing. The deal adds Meta to AMD's list of major clients, which already includes Google Cloud, Amazon Web Services, and Microsoft Azure.

feed.title: EPAM Systems (EPAM) Acquires Emakina, Boosts Digital Portfolio
feed.summary: EPAM Systems announced its acquisition of Belgium-based Emakina Group to enhance its digital solutions across the Europe, Middle East, and Africa markets. This strategic move is expected to broaden EPAM's digital and design portfolio, strengthening its capabilities in digital strategy, engineering, and creativity. Acquisitions have been a key growth driver for EPAM, allowing the company to expand its service offerings and global reach in the rapidly growing digital transformation market.

feed.title: Nvidia CEO: ‘We don’t have any magic bullets’ to deal with chip shortage
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that the global chip shortage is not expected to end soon, with demand continuing to outstrip supply through the next year. He noted that while Nvidia is fortunate to have diverse suppliers, there are no "magic bullets" to navigate the supply chain issues. Huang believes that the increased demand for chips, driven by factors like remote work and gaming, represents a permanent shift rather than a temporary trend.

feed.title: Samsung Announces Availability of Its Leading-Edge 2.5D Integration ‘H-Cube’ Solution for High Performance Applications
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics has announced the availability of its new Hybrid-Substrate Cube (H-Cube) technology, a 2.5D packaging solution for high-performance computing (HPC), AI, data center, and network products. Developed in collaboration with Samsung Electro-Mechanics and Amkor Technology, H-Cube uses an advanced silicon interposer and hybrid-substrate structure to efficiently integrate up to six HBMs, providing a cost-effective solution for large-area packaging challenges. This innovation seeks to address increasing integration requirements and substrate supply constraints in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Sony slashes PlayStation 5 production target amid chip shortage, supply chain challenges: report
feed.summary: Sony has reportedly lowered its PlayStation 5 production target for the current fiscal year due to global chip shortages and supply chain disruptions. The company now expects to produce about 15 million PS5s, down from an earlier target of over 16 million, impacting availability during the holiday season. Sony is investing in a new chip factory in Japan with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) to ease future supply constraints, while rival Nintendo has also revised its sales forecast downwards due to similar issues.

feed.title: COLORFUL Unveils GeForce RTX 3070 Customization Series Graphics Cards
feed.summary: COLORFUL has launched its new GeForce RTX 3070 Customization Series graphics cards, designed to replace its Bilibili Edition. The series includes the RTX 3070 and RTX 3070 Ti custom OC variants, featuring a two-sided cooling structure and magnetic interchangeable backplate covers for personalization. These cards are currently listed on COLORFUL's website but are expected to be available only in Chinese markets.

feed.title: Nvidia CEO Huang: We could make ARM successful in ‘all other areas of computing’
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang believes that even if the acquisition of ARM doesn't go through, Nvidia will continue to thrive by supporting a diverse range of CPUs, seeing opportunities for accelerated computing wherever CPUs are present. Huang also highlighted that integrating ARM with Nvidia could significantly boost ARM's research and development efforts, helping it succeed in various computing sectors beyond its current dominance in mobile devices. The deal faces significant regulatory hurdles in multiple countries, and its outcome will determine the future of the proposed merger.

feed.title: Podcast: Rivian's blockbuster IPO is a triumph of entrepreneurship
feed.summary: This podcast episode discusses Rivian's blockbuster $12 billion IPO, highlighting it as a significant entrepreneurial achievement. It also touches on whether a deal with Meta could help AMD compete more effectively with Intel. The article celebrates Rivian's success and explores potential shifts in the tech industry.

feed.title: Does Invesco’s new mutual fund offer investors a play on crypto? | Mint
feed.summary: Invesco Asset Management (India) is launching the Invesco CoinShares Global Blockchain ETF FoF, a feeder fund that invests in an Ireland-domiciled ETF. This underlying ETF invests in a mix of crypto-related and broader tech companies, offering Indian investors a partial and more conservative exposure to the cryptocurrency market. While it doesn't directly hold cryptocurrencies, its performance shows some correlation with Bitcoin's price movements, providing a way for investors curious about the space to gain exposure with less volatility than direct crypto investments.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to begin construction next year on new 300-mm semiconductor wafer fabrication plants
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TI) announced plans to begin construction in 2022 on new 300-millimeter semiconductor wafer fabrication plants ("fabs") in Sherman, Texas. This North Texas site could accommodate up to four fabs, with the first two starting construction next year. The investment, potentially reaching $30 billion, aims to meet future demand in industrial and automotive markets and strengthen TI's manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to build a $30 billion semiconductor chip manufacturing campus
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced plans for a $30 billion semiconductor chip manufacturing campus in Sherman, Texas, which could include up to four new plants. Construction on the first two 300-millimeter wafer fabrication plants is set to begin in 2022, with production starting by 2025. This significant investment aims to bolster the domestic semiconductor supply chain and create up to 3,000 jobs, with state and local officials highlighting Texas's competitive business environment as a key factor in the decision.

feed.title: Sabre (SABR) Collaborates With Mexico's Calafia Airlines
feed.summary: Sabre Corporation (SABR) has entered into a multi-year distribution partnership with Mexico's Calafia Airlines, allowing the airline to distribute its flights and services to travel agencies in the United States and other regions through Sabre's travel marketplace. This collaboration aims to accelerate Calafia Airlines' revenue growth and expand its global reach, while also helping Sabre strengthen its customer share in the Airlines Solutions segment. The deal is part of Sabre's broader strategy to upgrade its technology and support the recovery of the travel industry, following several other strategic partnerships with global airlines and hotel chains.

feed.title: Cover Story: Creating the Silicon Valley of the East 2.0
feed.summary: Penang, often called the Silicon Valley of the East due to its robust E&E ecosystem, is seeing its industrial landscape evolve with Batu Kawan emerging as a new, rapidly growing cluster. InvestPenang CEO Datuk Loo Lee Lian highlights the significant greenfield investments in Batu Kawan Industrial Park while also acknowledging the continued expansion in established areas like Bayan Lepas. The state aims to attract high-value companies that invest in talent and technology, diversify beyond E&E into areas like IC design and medical technology, and capitalize on global supply chain reconfigurations to maintain its competitive edge.

feed.title: Intel said to put off takeover of GlobalFoundries’ chip plant in China
feed.summary: Intel has reportedly shelved plans to acquire GlobalFoundries' abandoned chip plant in Chengdu, China. The acquisition, intended to expand Intel's operations and address the global chip shortage in China, was reportedly put on hold due to security concerns raised by the White House. Intel currently operates a chip fabrication plant in Dalian and two assembly and test facilities in Chengdu.

feed.title: NVIDIA CEO Receives Semiconductor Industry’s Top Honor
feed.summary: Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, was awarded the Semiconductor Industry Association's (SIA) highest honor, the Robert N. Noyce award, for his pioneering work in accelerated computing. The award ceremony highlighted NVIDIA's significant impact on the industry and AI development. Huang shared his journey from early programming to founding NVIDIA and his vision for advanced computing applications like Earth-2 for climate science.

feed.title: Apple’s First GaN Charger
feed.summary: Apple has introduced its first gallium nitride (GaN) charger, a 140W model for the new 16-inch MacBook Pro, marking a significant shift in power transistor technology for the company. TechInsights research confirms that GaN Systems secured the design win for this charger, highlighting the growing adoption of GaN in power electronics. This move by Apple, while not the first in the market for GaN, is expected to accelerate its integration into various applications, especially given the charger's use of the new USB Power Delivery 3.1 standard.

feed.title: MediaTek, AMD co-developed Wi-Fi 6E modules to power laptops, desktops from 2022
feed.summary: MediaTek and AMD have partnered to develop Wi-Fi 6E modules, the AMD RZ600 Series, which integrate MediaTek’s Filogic 330P chipset. These modules, designed to power AMD Ryzen-series laptops and desktops starting in 2022, aim to provide seamless connectivity, high throughput, and extended battery life. The Filogic 330P chipset supports Wi-Fi 6 (2.4/5GHz), 6E (6GHz band), and Bluetooth 5.2, also incorporating MediaTek’s power amplifier and low noise amplifier technology.

feed.title: Invesco ESG S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive profile of the Invesco ESG S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSPE), detailing its price, dividends, expense ratio, holdings, and ESG performance. It outlines key vitals such as its inception date, index tracked, and AUM, alongside comparisons with alternative ETFs in its category. The report also includes volatility analysis, technical indicators, and recent news related to the ETF.

feed.title: Habana Labs AI Training Solution Featuring Supermicro X12 Gaudi AI Training Server With DDN AI400X2 Storage System
feed.summary: Habana Labs, an Intel company, has announced a new enterprise AI training solution that combines the Supermicro X12 Gaudi AI training server with the DDN AI400X2 storage system. This collaboration aims to provide a cost-efficient, scalable, and easy-to-use solution that eliminates storage bottlenecks and optimizes AI compute capacity utilization. The integrated system is designed to address the increasing demand for AI training and model rebuilding while keeping costs down for enterprise customers.

feed.title: Amazon's cloud unit launches new chips to take on Intel, Nvidia
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has introduced two new custom computing chips, Graviton3 and Trainium, aimed at competing with offerings from Intel, AMD, and Nvidia. AWS claims its new chips offer better performance per dollar and lower costs for machine learning model training compared to its rivals. This move signals AWS's continued drive to develop its own hardware and intensify competition in the cloud computing and chip markets.

feed.title: eInfochips to expand Hyderabad unit
feed.summary: eInfochips, a provider of product engineering and semiconductor design services, is expanding its Hyderabad design center to meet growing customer demand. The company plans to hire approximately 100 engineers in the next six months and aims for a total of 250 new engineers by FY2023, focusing on silicon design, verification, and physical design. This expansion will enhance eInfochips' global capabilities in ASIC & FPGA design, verification, and validation.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise CEO says navigating the chip shortage won't be easy — but end is in sight
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise CEO Antonio Neri believes the global chip shortage will start to ease after the summer of 2022 due to new fabrication plants coming online. While acknowledging the difficulty of navigating the current supply chain issues, Neri emphasized HPE's strategies, including diversified suppliers and adaptable product designs, to manage component availability. Major chipmakers are also building new factories, although these will take several years to become operational.

feed.title: ASE to Dispose Holdings in Subsidiaries to Wise Road Capital
feed.summary: ASE Technology Holding Co. Ltd announced its agreement to sell shares and equity interests in GAPT Holding Ltd and ASE (Kunshan) Inc. to Beijing Wise Road Asset Management Co. Ltd. The transaction, valued at $1.46 billion, will involve a phased cash payment and is expected to result in a $630 million tax-effected gain for ASE. This strategic move aims to optimize ASE's resource allocation in China, enhance investments in advanced technology, and expand its capacity in Taiwan.

feed.title: U.S. sues to block Nvidia’s $40-billion chip takeover of Arm
feed.summary: U.S. antitrust officials have sued to block Nvidia's proposed $40-billion acquisition of Arm Ltd., citing concerns that the deal would stifle innovation and undermine competition in the semiconductor industry. The Federal Trade Commission argues that the merger would grant Nvidia excessive control over chip designs widely used across various technologies. The acquisition, initially valued at $40 billion in September 2020, has faced regulatory hurdles globally due to Arm's critical role as a technology licensor to hundreds of companies including Nvidia's rivals.

feed.title: Synopsys Extends Industry Leadership as Customers Surpass 500 Tapeouts Using Flagship Fusion Compiler Solution
feed.summary: Synopsys announced that its flagship Fusion Compiler RTL-to-GDSII solution has been used for over 500 tapeouts since its general availability in 2019. Customers leveraging Fusion Compiler have reported significant performance improvements, including up to 20% better performance, 15% lower power, and 5% smaller area compared to competitive solutions. The solution has been adopted by leading semiconductor companies for designs ranging from 40nm to 3nm across key markets like 5G mobile, HPC, AI, and hyperscale data centers.

feed.title: Regulators in US, UK, EU All Alarmed By Chip Maker Nvidia’s $40B Deal to Buy Chip Designer
feed.summary: Regulators in the U.S., U.K., and E.U. are expressing alarm over Nvidia Corporation's $40 billion proposed acquisition of chip designer Arm Ltd. The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has filed a lawsuit to block the deal, citing concerns that it would grant Nvidia too much power and stifle competition and innovation in the semiconductor industry. Nvidia, however, maintains that the merger would benefit the industry, promote competition, and preserve Arm's open licensing model.

feed.title: Is Texas America’s Rising Semiconductor Hotspot?
feed.summary: Texas is rapidly becoming a major hub for semiconductor development, attracting large corporations like Samsung and Tesla due to its business-friendly climate, lack of corporate and personal income taxes, and abundant skilled workforce. The state boasts a robust academic system and ample space for development, making it an ideal location for the expansion of semiconductor manufacturing. With significant investments from companies like Texas Instruments and Samsung, coupled with state and potential federal incentives, Texas aims to solidify its position as a self-sufficient leader in the semiconductor industry and create thousands of new jobs.

feed.title: Nvidia metaverse future bright even as Arm may slip from grasp
feed.summary: Nvidia's acquisition of British chip technology provider Arm faces significant hurdles, with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission suing to block the deal and analysts doubting its completion. Despite this potential setback, Nvidia's future prospects remain strong, driven by its focus on artificial intelligence (AI) and the metaverse, areas where it is a leading player. The company's data center business is thriving as internet companies invest heavily in augmented and virtual reality technologies.

feed.title: Infographic: Highlights of 3M Company’s Q3 2021 earnings report
feed.summary: Shares of The 3M Company (NYSE: MMM) rebounded last week after reporting stronger-than-expected Q3 2021 financial results. The company's adjusted earnings were flat year-over-year at $2.45 per share but topped expectations, while net sales increased 7% to $8.94 billion. Management narrowed its fiscal 2021 earnings guidance and expects net sales to grow 9-10%.

feed.title: AMD Short Interest Rises To 71 Million Shares & CEO Sells Close To $36 Million Of Stock
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has seen its short interest increase to 71.6 million shares by the end of November, despite a previous decline since July. This rise coincides with an 8% dip in AMD's share price in November, although the stock is still up 50% year-to-date. Concurrently, AMD's CEO, Dr. Lisa Su, and other executives have sold approximately $36 million worth of company stock in November and early December.

feed.title: ASML talks about its High NA EUV machines, coming in 2023
feed.summary: ASML, a crucial supplier of lithographic machines for the semiconductor industry, is preparing to introduce its next-generation High NA EUV machines. These machines are expected to enable significantly smaller chip features and increased chip density, leading to more advanced and sophisticated chips. Initial trials are anticipated in 2023, with volume production by 2025, and Intel is slated to be the first customer.

feed.title: Corsair Dominator Platinum RGB DDR5 RAM review: Same gorgeous modules with next-gen internals
feed.summary: This article reviews Corsair's Dominator Platinum RGB DDR5 RAM, highlighting its design, performance, and features. While it offers solid reliability at 5200MT/s with impressive RGB lighting and iCUE software support, it's noted to be expensive and not a significant performance upgrade over high-end DDR4 RAM in its early stages. The review concludes that it's a good option for those building a new PC for 12th Gen Intel processors and wanting the latest technology, despite DDR5 being in its infancy.

feed.title: Wise Road Drops $1.4 Billion Buyout of Magnachip
feed.summary: Chinese private equity firm Wise Road Capital has terminated its $1.4 billion all-cash merger agreement with U.S. chipmaker Magnachip Semiconductor Corp. The termination was due to the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) failing to approve the deal, citing national security risks. Wise Road Capital will pay Magnachip a $70.2 million termination fee.

feed.title: Tata Group in talks with Taiwan companies to make chips in India
feed.summary: The Tata Group is engaging with major international companies, including those from Taiwan, to venture into semiconductor chip manufacturing in India. This move comes as India largely imports chips and the Tata Group, with its presence in automobile and renewable power sectors, has a significant captive demand for semiconductors. The Indian government is also actively promoting semiconductor manufacturing through production-linked incentive schemes to boost domestic production.

feed.title: Intel to invest $7 billion in new plant in Malaysia, creating 9,000 jobs
feed.summary: Intel Chief Executive Pat Gelsinger announced a $7 billion investment to build a new chip-packaging and testing factory in Malaysia, creating over 4,000 Intel jobs and more than 5,000 construction jobs. This expansion aims to address the global semiconductor shortage, which Gelsinger expects to persist into 2023. The new advanced packaging facility is projected to begin production in 2024.

feed.title: HSBC raises Taiwan outlook over semiconductor industry
feed.summary: HSBC's global private banking and wealth management division has an optimistic outlook for Taiwan, primarily due to the anticipated continued growth of the semiconductor sector driven by global digitalization. While semiconductor earnings per share growth might slow in the coming year due to a high comparison base, demand is expected to remain strong, especially with new applications for the metaverse. However, PGIM Securities Investment Trust Enterprise Co projected single-digit profit growth for local companies next year, with the electronics sector performing well but conventional industries facing rising raw material costs and the shipping sector struggling with persistent port congestion.

feed.title: Desktop computers and All-in-One PCs
feed.summary: HP offers a wide range of desktop computers and All-in-One PCs designed for various uses, including work, entertainment, gaming, and creative tasks. The article highlights new AI PC models like the HP OmniDesk and OmniStudio X, alongside established lines such as HP Pavilion, Envy, OMEN, and VICTUS, detailing their specifications and intended applications. Customers can filter choices by use case, series, operating system, product type, and screen size to find the perfect device.

feed.title: TSMC introduces new chip technology for datacenters
feed.summary: TSMC has introduced a new semiconductor manufacturing technology called N4X, specifically designed for High Performance Computing (HPC) products. This new process, part of the N5 family, offers significant performance boosts and leverages TSMC's 5nm volume production experience. HPC is identified as TSMC's fastest-growing business segment, and the N4X is expected to enter risk production by the first half of 2023.

feed.title: 3D Machine Vision Market size worth $ 2.55 Billion, Globally, by 2028 at 10.02% CAGR: Verified Market Research®
feed.summary: The global 3D Machine Vision Market was valued at USD 1.45 Billion in 2020 and is projected to reach USD 2.55 Billion by 2028, exhibiting a CAGR of 10.02% from 2021 to 2028. This growth is driven by increasing demand for quality inspection and automation, particularly in industrial sectors and vision-guided robotic systems. The market is also benefiting from technological advancements, such as OMRON Corporation's FH-series of Vision System, and the growing need for automated quality assurance in a post-COVID-19 manufacturing landscape.

feed.title: Intel i9-12900K Ties AMD Ryzen 9 5950X in World-Record Cinebench R20 Run
feed.summary: Intel's Core i9-12900K has achieved a world-record Cinebench R20 score of 15,664 points, tying with the AMD Ryzen 9 5950X, a record previously set by the same overclocker, Splave, nearly a year prior. This tie is considered unprecedented given the significant architectural differences between the two processors, including Intel's hybrid core layout and DDR5 memory. Both records were achieved using extreme liquid nitrogen cooling.

feed.title: HKSTP, Avnet, ORIX Nurture GBA Startups
feed.summary: Hong Kong Science and Technology Parks Corporation (HKSTP) has announced strategic partnerships with Avnet and ORIX to support emerging startups in advanced hardware and digital technologies within the Greater Bay Area (GBA). These collaborations aim to provide crucial resources such as design expertise, market access, funding, and R&D support to accelerate the growth of startups and establish the GBA as a leading innovation hub. The initiative targets challenges faced by startups in funding and market access, with a particular focus on leveraging Hong Kong's proximity to manufacturing powerhouses like Shenzhen.
feed.source: Hong Kong Science & Technology Parks Corporation
feed.source_domain: Hong Kong Science & Technology Parks Corporation

feed.title: 17 Semiconductor Companies Forecast to Have >$10B Sales in 2021
feed.summary: According to IC Insights, 17 companies are projected to achieve over $10 billion in worldwide semiconductor sales in 2021, with AMD, NXP Semiconductors, and Analog Devices joining this "megasuppliers" list. The combined sales of these companies are expected to grow by 26% year-over-year, outpacing the overall industry's 25% growth. Samsung is forecast to be the largest semiconductor supplier, with sales nearing $83.1 billion, driven by a strong memory market.

feed.title: Crucial MX500 4TB Solid State Drive - Reviews - all-pages
feed.summary: This article reviews the Crucial MX500 4TB SSD, highlighting its features, performance, and endurance. It details the drive's specifications, including its use of Silicon Motion's SM2259 controller and Micron's 176-layer 3D TLC NAND flash, and presents benchmark results from various tests like CrystalDiskMark, HD Tach, and PCMark. The review concludes that the 4TB MX500 remains a strong contender in the SATA SSD market, offering good performance, advanced features, and a competitive price.

feed.title: AMD amends GlobalFoundries deal, will buy $2.1 bln worth of wafers
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) has amended its agreement with GlobalFoundries, committing to purchase approximately $2.1 billion worth of silicon wafers between 2022 and 2025. This updated deal expands upon a previous agreement to buy $1.6 billion worth of chips from 2022 to 2024. Although AMD relies on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co for its most critical "chiplets," GlobalFoundries, a former AMD spin-off, continues to supply components to tie their chips together.

feed.title: Intel Apologizes for Asking Suppliers to Avoid Xinjiang
feed.summary: Intel Corp. issued an apology after asking its suppliers to avoid sourcing goods from Xinjiang, a region where the Chinese government is accused of human rights abuses. This request, made to comply with U.S. regulations, drew sharp criticism from Chinese state media and prompted calls for boycotts of Intel products. The incident highlights the growing tensions between foreign companies and China over the Xinjiang issue.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: AMD and GlobalFoundries Renew their Wafer Supply Agreement to increase their wafer commitments
feed.summary: AMD and GlobalFoundries have renewed their wafer supply agreement, extending it to 2025 and increasing the confirmed spending by $500 million to a total of $2.1 billion. This agreement secures more guaranteed manufacturing capacity for AMD, primarily for 12nm and 14nm wafers. The extension ensures GlobalFoundries continues to supply AMD with critical components for its Ryzen and EPYC processors.
